differences.title: 1. PRIMARY USE CASE
differences.leftDesc: Optimized for finding similar items based on meaning, not exact matches.
differences.rightDesc: Designed for structured data, transactions, and complex queries.

differences.title: 2. DATA STRUCTURE
differences.leftDesc: Stores data as high-dimensional numerical vectors representing content.
differences.rightDesc: Organizes data into predefined tables with fixed columns and rows.

differences.title: 3. QUERYING MECHANISM
differences.leftDesc: Efficiently finds items that are 'close' to a query vector.
differences.rightDesc: Uses Structured Query Language for precise data retrieval and manipulation.

differences.title: 4. SCALABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Handles massive amounts of vector data and similarity queries effectively.
differences.rightDesc: Scalability depends heavily on design, hardware, and specific RDBMS.

differences.title: 5. PERFORMANCE FOCUS
differences.leftDesc: Prioritizes finding relevant results quickly for AI applications.
differences.rightDesc: Emphasizes accuracy, consistency, and ACID compliance for transactions.

differences.title: 6. SCHEMA FLEXIBILITY
differences.leftDesc: Embeddings can represent diverse data types without strict predefined structures.
differences.rightDesc: Requires a well-defined schema before data can be inserted.

differences.title: 7. PRIMARY USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Recommender systems, image search, semantic text search, anomaly detection.
differences.rightDesc: Customer data, inventory management, financial records, order processing.
